findings.title: Autoscroll animates on every streamed token
findings.details: LaunchedEffect key is (messages.size, messages.lastOrNull()?.text?.length). During streaming the text length changes on every token (10-30 Hz), so animateScrollToItem is re-triggered on every emission. animateScrollToItem launches a spring animation that is immediately cancelled by the next call, creating visual jitter and wasted work on the main thread. Gallery's LlmChatView uses scrollToItem (non-animated) for streaming and animateScrollToItem only when a new message is appended.
findings.recommendation: Split into two effects or branch on message count vs text growth. Example: use scrollToItem for streaming updates and animateScrollToItem only when messages.size changes. Or key only on messages.size + whether last message is streaming, and call scrollToItem when streaming.

findings.title: ApplicationContext injected into ViewModel only to resolve a formatted string
findings.details: @ApplicationContext is injected solely to call context.getString(R.string.ttft_footer_format, ...) inside the resultListener. Holding Context inside a ViewModel is a known friction point (hard to unit-test, couples VM to Android framework) and patterns.md emphasises module-boundary cleanliness. The footer could be modelled as raw numbers (ttftMs, totalSec) inside the Message data class and formatted in the Composable via stringResource — keeping the VM pure Kotlin.
findings.recommendation: Move footer formatting to the UI layer: extend Message with ttftMs: Long? and totalSec: Double? (or a small MessageStats type), drop the Context dependency, and format in MessageBubble using stringResource(R.string.ttft_footer_format, stats.ttftMs, stats.totalSec). Leaves ViewModel free of Android resource lookups and easier to test later.

findings.title: Error callback schedules logging on viewModelScope — may drop on VM clear
findings.details: onError launches errorLog.e on viewModelScope. If the user navigates away mid-inference, viewModelScope is cancelled before the logger writes to disk, and the inference failure goes unlogged even though patterns.md mandates an ERROR [inference] line for every inference failure. onCleared's dedicated SupervisorJob scope exists precisely to outlive the VM; the same reasoning applies to this terminal log write.
findings.recommendation: Use an application-scoped logger scope (SupervisorJob + Dispatchers.IO, either shared via Hilt or created inline like in onCleared) for the errorLog.e call. Alternatively, since ErrorLog.e is already non-throwing, it is acceptable to launch on GlobalScope in this narrow case — but a shared injected scope is cleaner.

findings.title: Late resultListener emissions can overwrite interrupted assistant text
findings.details: After stop() flips the last assistant message to interrupted=true and streaming=false, the helper's resultListener may still deliver trailing partial chunks (the litertlm engine cannot guarantee zero post-stop emissions within 500ms). updateLastAssistant on partial will keep appending tokens to the bubble after 'прервано' was shown, producing a visually inconsistent end-state (text grows after the user pressed Stop). The interrupted flag survives copy(), but the text diverges.
findings.recommendation: Gate the resultListener on a local isCancelled flag set by stop(), or check _uiState.value inside the listener: if (state is Ready && !state.isGenerating) return. Cheap and defensive.

findings.title: firstTokenMs stored as Long in footer — conversion to Int may truncate on >2^31 ms
findings.details: ttftMs.toInt() is used because the string format uses %1$dms. 2^31 ms = ~24 days, so truncation is not realistic, but the format spec in strings.xml uses %d (Int). If the footer ever shows a negative value after a long-stalled first-token, the root cause will be this implicit Long→Int narrowing.
findings.recommendation: Either change the string format to %1$d and pass Long via boxing (Kotlin autoboxes Long for varargs), or leave as-is and add a code comment explaining the truncation is bounded by practical TTFT < 60s. Non-blocking.

findings.title: sealed interface ChatUiState lives at file top — style consistency with project
findings.details: ChatUiState is declared as a top-level sealed interface in the same file as ChatViewModel. Task 8's ModelManagerViewModel pattern (if followed) might put UiState inside a companion object or nested in the VM. Verify consistency with existing VMs for cohesion. Not a correctness issue.
findings.recommendation: Compare with ModelManagerViewModel.kt — if the project convention is nested state, nest ChatUiState. If top-level is the convention, keep as is.

findings.title: send() gets model AFTER the initial guard but does not re-check isGenerating before mutation
findings.details: send() is only invoked from the UI IconButton when isGenerating=false (button shows Send instead of Stop), and the guard on line 67 rejects concurrent sends. Given Compose recomposition cannot invoke send twice within the same frame, this is safe — noted only for completeness.

summary: Task 9 implementation is well-structured, matches the tech-spec contract, and correctly integrates with ModelRegistry / LlmModelHelper / ErrorLog (all cross-file signatures verified). Strings are fully externalised via the canonical 9-key set. No critical or high-severity issues. Two medium findings (autoscroll jitter on every streamed token, ApplicationContext coupling in VM) are worth addressing before Phase 2 UI polish; the low/info findings are defensive cleanups. Status: approved with suggestions.
